Surface-roughness readings (µm) measured by 4 operators on 5 components of the same type. Apply Chauvenet's exclusion principle to flag possible outliers. Fill the yellow cells in column C.
Data
Chauvenet's exclusion principle
Chauvenet's exclusion principle
Surface roughness readings (µm), n = 20
Sample average xbar =
Sample standard deviation s =
Number of data n =
Risk alpha = 1/(2n) =
Limit factor z = NORM.S.INV(1-1/(4n)) =
Exclusion lower limit =
Exclusion upper limit =
Outlier detected? (1=Yes, 0=No)
Outlier value =
Remember: use STDEV.S, not STDEV.P. For Chauvenet, alpha is a probability. The z quantile uses 1 - 1/(4n), because the two-sided risk is split across two tails.

Use HYPGEOM.DIST(k; sample_size; successes_in_population; population_size; cumulative). For phase B, the population has shrunk by the Phase-A sample size, and the remaining defectives are the original defectives minus the defects already found in A.
